title: Module 0 Project: New Inventory Intake
summary: Add a five-item approved inventory batch through five cumulative INSERT steps.

sketchBlocks.title: Introducing the Inventory Intake Project
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you will take on the role of a Junior Data Operations Analyst tasked with adding a batch of new inventory products to the database. The goal is to insert these records without relying on column order or accidentally replacing explicit values with defaults. This project will help you understand the importance of using explicit column lists and how to apply NULL and DEFAULT values correctly. By the end of this project, you will have a cumulative SQL script that inserts a clean five-item batch with explicit columns, intentional NULL values, controlled defaults, and verification SELECT statements.

quizDraft.title: Add the First Approved Item
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new item, Desk Organizer, into the inventory with explicit columns and values.
quizDraft.hint: Use an explicit column list to insert the Desk Organizer with all required details.

help.concept: Explicit column lists ensure that each value is inserted into the correct column, preventing errors due to column order changes.
help.hint_1: List all columns explicitly in the INSERT statement.
help.hint_2: Ensure each value matches the intended column, including id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, and last_updated.

quizDraft.title: Record an Item with No Notes
quizDraft.prompt: Insert Cable Labels into the inventory, ensuring the notes column is set to NULL.
quizDraft.hint: Use SQL NULL for the notes column to indicate no notes are available.

help.concept: Using SQL NULL allows you to represent missing or unknown data in a column.
help.hint_1: Include NULL in the VALUES list for the notes column.
help.hint_2: Ensure the notes column is explicitly listed in the INSERT statement.

quizDraft.title: Use the Approved Defaults
quizDraft.prompt: Insert Shipping Pouch into the inventory, omitting status and last_updated to use defaults.
quizDraft.hint: Omit the status and last_updated columns to use their default values.

help.concept: Omitting columns with defaults in an INSERT statement allows the database to use the declared defaults.
help.hint_1: Do not include status and last_updated in the column list.
help.hint_2: Ensure the remaining columns are explicitly listed and values are provided.

quizDraft.title: Add Two Items as One Batch
quizDraft.prompt: Insert Packing Tape and Label Roll in one multi-row INSERT statement.
quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Insert the fourth and fifth approved items
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES 
(104, 'Packing Tape', 'Office Supplies', 2.99, 'active', 0, 'Strong adhesive', '2026-02-12'),
(105, 'Label Roll', 'Office Supplies', 4.49, 'active', 0, 'For labeling', '2026-02-12');

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (104, 105);

starterFiles.content:
-- Insert the fourth and fifth approved items
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES 
(104, 'Packing Tape', 'Office Supplies', 2.99, 'active', 0, 'Strong adhesive', '2026-02-12'),
(105, 'Label Roll', 'Office Supplies', 4.49, 'active', 0, 'For labeling', '2026-02-12');

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (104, 105);

solutionFiles.content:
-- Insert the fourth and fifth approved items
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES 
(104, 'Packing Tape', 'Office Supplies', 2.99, 'active', 0, 'Strong adhesive', '2026-02-12'),
(105, 'Label Roll', 'Office Supplies', 4.49, 'active', 0, 'For labeling', '2026-02-12');

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (104, 105);

quizDraft.solutionCode:
-- Insert the fourth and fifth approved items
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES 
(104, 'Packing Tape', 'Office Supplies', 2.99, 'active', 0, 'Strong adhesive', '2026-02-12'),
(105, 'Label Roll', 'Office Supplies', 4.49, 'active', 0, 'For labeling', '2026-02-12');

-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (104, 105);

tests.stdout:
104|Packing Tape|Office Supplies|2.99|active|0|Strong adhesive|2026-02-12
105|Label Roll|Office Supplies|4.49|active|0|For labeling|2026-02-12

quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (104, 105);
quizDraft.hint: Use a multi-row INSERT to add both items with the same column list.

help.concept: A multi-row INSERT allows you to add multiple records in a single statement, improving efficiency.
help.hint_1: List all columns explicitly and provide values for both items in the VALUES clause.
help.hint_2: Ensure each set of values corresponds to the correct columns.

quizDraft.title: Finish the Intake Handoff
quizDraft.prompt: Ensure all five new items are inserted correctly and verify the entire inventory remains consistent.

tests.stdout:
1|Sticker Pack|Accessories|4.99|active|0|NULL|2026-02-01
2|Test Mug|Kitchen|7.5|draft|1|Internal sample|2026-02-03
3|Canvas Print|Decor|45.0|active|0|NULL|2026-02-02
4|Sample Poster|Decor|12.0|inactive|1|Archived test item|2026-01-29
5|Water Bottle|Kitchen|15.0|active|0|NULL|2026-02-05
6|Trial Tote|Accessories|9.0|draft|1|Trial inventory|2026-02-06
101|Desk Organizer|Office Supplies|12.99|active|0|New stock|2026-02-10
102|Cable Labels|Office Supplies|3.99|active|0|NULL|2026-02-11
103|Shipping Pouch|Packaging|5.99|draft|0|Durable material|2026-02-01
104|Packing Tape|Office Supplies|2.99|active|0|Strong adhesive|2026-02-12
105|Label Roll|Office Supplies|4.49|active|0|For labeling|2026-02-12

quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id BETWEEN 101 AND 105;
quizDraft.hint: Verify that all new items are present and existing items remain unchanged.

help.concept: Verification ensures that the intended changes have been applied correctly without affecting existing data.
help.hint_1: Use SELECT to verify the presence of all new items by their ids.
help.hint_2: Check that the existing inventory items are unchanged.
